area_anchor: Reception inbox should maintain a live view without staff pressing refresh or sync buttons
current_truth: The Reception inbox used to require explicit operator actions to feel current. The UI refresh path was recently separated from Gmail sync, but new mail still depended on a manual sync action, and the screen still depended on staff clicking buttons to reload visible data.
next_scope_now: Implement a bounded live-inbox first wave: scheduled inbox sync on the worker, visible-tab auto-refresh against the D1-backed inbox list, and draft-safe refresh behavior that avoids clobbering in-progress edits.
adjacent_later: Denormalize inbox list fields on threads to remove per-row subqueries from the list query
Add selective detail refresh only when the selected thread summary changes
Evaluate push invalidation after the polling path proves stable in production
evidence_refs: operator-stated: the inbox should maintain a live view without staff pressing buttons
why: Reception staff should not need to remember which button to press to keep the inbox current. A hostel inbox should surface new work automatically and refresh the visible queue in the background without disrupting draft editing.

intended_outcome.statement: Reception automatically syncs Gmail into D1 on a schedule and refreshes the visible inbox list in the browser while the tab is active, reducing manual refresh work without overwriting in-progress draft edits.

completed_by.outcome: Reception now auto-refreshes the inbox list every 15 seconds while the tab is visible and online, and the worker can auto-sync Gmail into D1 every minute via an authenticated internal cron route without requiring staff to press Sync.
